name: open-forge
description: Self-host open-source apps on your own cloud, your own VPS, your own Kubernetes cluster, a PaaS, or your own laptop. Chat-friendly interface to existing tools (aws, az, hcloud, doctl, gcloud, flyctl, kubectl, helm, podman, docker, lume, tailscale, ssh, gh) — Claude runs every command; you only answer what/where/how and provide credentials. Supported software: Ghost, OpenClaw, Hermes-Agent, Ollama. For OpenClaw and Hermes-Agent, every upstream-blessed install path documented at docs.openclaw.ai/install/* and docs.hermes-agent's getting-started/* is covered. For Ollama (local-LLM inference server, the foundation layer for self-hosted AI), every install method is covered: native (Linux/macOS/Windows installers, manual extracts), Docker (CPU + NVIDIA CUDA + AMD ROCm + Vulkan tags), Kubernetes via community Helm chart, plus Homebrew, Nix/NixOS module, and Pacman. Composes with OpenClaw / Hermes / Open WebUI / LibreChat / Aider as an OpenAI-compatible provider.
